summary: Protagonist with rationalist/analytical framework resists supernatural involvement until social pressure from ally figure forces initial engagement, establishing thematic tension between worldview and evidence
evidence_scope: Volume 1 chapter 1-3; repeated across subsequent arcs with geographic/circumstantial variation

summary: Physical discovery followed by emotional/supernatural response creates two-part payoff structure; investigation sequences compound evidence accumulation before deductive leap, delaying major revelation across multiple chapters

summary: Investigation rationalism maintained as scaffolding until deliberate tone shatter; horror introduced during emotional vulnerability peak or analytical focus creating maximum impact; transition signaled through prose register and sentence rhythm compression

summary: Touch, temperature, and sound precede visual confirmation; physical sensations (trembling, cold crawl, throat constriction) externalize psychological states; supernatural elements described through physiological response before explicit threat statement
evidence_scope: Universal horror sequences; environmental sensory disruption across all volumes

summary: Supernatural threat introduced through mundane item (mirror, clock, apple, cat) becoming focal horror element; ordinary object distorted, absent, or wrong before entity revelation
evidence_scope: Volume 1 mirror; Volume 2 apple obsession; Volume 4 copper lion orientation

summary: Specific countdown (diagnosis timeline) transforms passive death sentence into active mission framework; emotional stakes coexist with investigation urgency; protagonist becomes agent rather than victim

summary: Character with established malevolent reputation reveals hidden benevolent intent through self-sacrificing protection; apparent threat conceals saving mechanism

summary: Cold, dread, wrong sounds, missing light precede supernatural entity introduction; sensory anomaly accumulation before explicit threat statement; atmosphere built through absence
